element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players establish directional control and basic attack
Limited resource strategy and crowd control

element_introduction_order.element: Weapon upgrades (Assault Rifle, Shotgun, Grenade Launcher, Flamethrower)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tactical weapon selection based on enemy types and stage layout
Alternate mechanics and different movement/damage paradigm

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension with moment-to-moment resource scarcity; arcade pacing emphasizes continuous threat with pickup-driven relief cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to core movement and basic gunplay; weapon variety discovered early to establish player agency
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy density increases; vehicle sections introduce mechanical variation; weapon choice becomes tactically important
difficulty_curve.late_game: High enemy concentration; Megacrush resource management critical; final level presents president rescue objective as climactic scenario
notable_spikes: Transition from standard combat to vehicle sections (change in control feel)
Level 6 to Level 7 final boss (Hercules Transport encounter)

notable_level_patterns.description: Six standard campaign levels plus final rescue level; each stage advances narrative toward president rescue in fictional African nation Zutula

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Weapon shop integration (Genesis Original Mode exclusive)
notable_level_patterns.description: Original Mode for Genesis adds Weapon Shops between stages where players spend currency dropped from defeated enemies to purchase permanent upgrades and character switches
Certain stages allow player to commandeer enemy vehicles, shifting from foot-based movement to vehicle-based combat with different control properties

sources_used: wikipedia: Mercs article - Gameplay section, Ports section (Genesis Original Mode details)
